action.hypothesis: Round 65 established `two_stage_residual` as the new mainline by materially beating the previous `gated` K128 control under the matched `signum`/`2.0e-3` recipe, so the highest-value next step is to convert that one-off probe into a clean mainline baseline before touching learning rate or optimizer. This round answers one question only: does the same `two_stage_residual` K128 EF12 `signum` `2.0e-3` recipe reproduce a similarly strong curve as a stable baseline worth tuning from, or was Round 65 an overoptimistic single run.
action.summary: Run a clean baseline repeat for the new `two_stage_residual` mainline at `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `LR=2.0e-3`, changing no recipe variable beyond promoting the family into baseline confirmation.
action.self_review: This follows the mainline progression rule by staying within the same family and using the recommended next step of establishing a clean baseline before any LR or optimizer change. The round changes no substantive training variable, so the result will be easy to attribute and will tell us whether the promising staged-residual curve is robust enough to tune further.
notes_to_memory: Round 66 promotes `two_stage_residual` from matched probe winner to mainline candidate by running a clean baseline repeat before any recipe tuning.
Judge this round primarily by reproducibility of the training curve shape and final FVU relative to Round 65, not just by whether the endpoint is slightly above or below 0.1278.

If the baseline reproduces cleanly, the next mainline question should be a single learning-rate adjustment inside `two_stage_residual`; if it fails to reproduce, investigate stability before changing optimizer or K.
next_hypotheses: If the clean baseline reproduces the strong Round 65 curve, test one learning-rate adjustment within `two_stage_residual` next.
If the clean baseline is materially worse or less stable, rerun only if needed to separate variance from recipe instability before changing optimizer.
If the curve is strong early but plateaus oddly, use the next round for a single LR change rather than touching K or architecture.

result.description: Run a clean baseline repeat for the new `two_stage_residual` mainline at `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `LR=2.0e-3`, changing no recipe variable beyond promoting the family into baseline confirmation.
result.self_review: This follows the mainline progression rule by staying within the same family and using the recommended next step of establishing a clean baseline before any LR or optimizer change. The round changes no substantive training variable, so the result will be easy to attribute and will tell us whether the promising staged-residual curve is robust enough to tune further.
